briefdescription.para: squared distance between two points, which is faster to compute than just distance
distance between two points, better use distanceSq for higher performance

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector3< T > &a, const Vector3< T > &b, const Vector3< T > &c)
memberdef.name: mixed

detaileddescription.para: computes minimal angle in [0,pi] between two vectors; the function is symmetric: angle( a, b ) == angle( b, a )

inbodydescription.para: this version is slower and less precise return std::acos( std::clamp( dot( a.normalized(), b.normalized() ), T(-1), T(1) ) );

briefdescription.para: returns a point on unit sphere given two angles
We don't need to bind those functions in Python, because this doesn't prevent __iter__ from being generated for the type.

memberdef.argsstring: (T x, T y, T z) noexcept
memberdef.name: Vector3

memberdef.argsstring: (std::constructible_from< T, U >) explicit const expr Vector3(const Vector2< U > &v) noexcept
memberdef.name: MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED

memberdef.argsstring: (!std::is_same_v< T, U >) const expr explicit Vector3(const Vector3< U > &v) noexcept
memberdef.name: MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED

detaileddescription.para: Here T == U doesn't seem to cause any issues in the C++ code, but we're still disabling it because it somehow gets emitted when generating the bindings, and looks out of place there.

inbodydescription.para: Calling sqrt this way to hopefully support boost.multiprecision numbers. Returning auto to not break on integral types.

memberdef.type: Vector3 furthestBasisVector() const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std std::pair< Vector3, Vector3 > perpendicular() const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std Vector3
memberdef.definition: Vector3 furthestBasisVector() const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std std::pair< Vector3, Vector3 > perpendicular() const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(std Vector3 MR::Vector3< T >::transformed
memberdef.name: transformed

briefdescription.para: returns one of 3 basis unit vector that makes the biggest angle with the direction specified by this

detaileddescription.para: returns 2 unit vector, which together with this vector make an orthogonal basis Currently not implemented for integral vectors. returns this vector transformed by xf if it is Need this, otherwise the bindings try to instantiate AffineXf3 with non-FP arguments.

briefdescription.para: get rid of signed zero values to be sure that equal vectors have identical binary representation
NOTE: We use std::declval() in the operators below because libclang 18 in our binding generator is bugged and chokes on decltyping a.x and such. TODO fix this when we update libclang.

memberdef.definition: Vector3< decltype(std::declval< T >() - std::declval< T >())> operator-
memberdef.name: operator-
